As of the cutoff, the relevant Wikipedia list was still using a pre-cutoff October 2025 revision, and the background says the most recent known status on 2025-10-30 was 'No'. Sepsis is also a syndrome caused by many different infections, so a clean flip to 'Yes' would likely require either a major, very visible biomedical breakthrough or a substantial Wikipedia reinterpretation. The available pre-cutoff context points the other way: sepsis organizations and WHO discuss vaccines as preventing infections that can lead to sepsis, not a direct sepsis vaccine, while candidate products appear to still be in development. Over only 1 week, 1 month, and ~3 months, a change to 'Yes' is possible but quite unlikely; I raise the probability slightly with horizon to reflect more time for a breakthrough or Wikipedia update.

That makes a Wikipedia-recognized vaccine appearing within 1 week, 1 month, or even ~3 months very unlikely, though the longer horizon gets slightly more probability and thus closer to 0.5.

Wikipedia’s list page as of 2025-10-01 shows Capillariasis with no vaccine listed, and the question background says the latest known status as of 2025-10-30 was still No. Outside view: this is a rare neglected parasitic helminth disease, a category in which human vaccines are very uncommon and almost never appear over a span of days to a few months. The one additional search mainly surfaced CDC and review material focused on antiparasitic treatment and food-safety prevention, not vaccine development. So YES by mid-November looks extremely unlikely; the probability rises only slightly by December and February because longer horizons allow a bit more chance of a breakthrough or Wikipedia update, but there is still no visible pipeline signal before the cutoff.

Finke is the current listed holder at 14:30.67, and the strongest recent test of the record—the 2025 World Championships—did not come especially close: Ahmed Jaouadi won in 14:34.41, with Sven Schwarz at 14:35.69. This event’s world record is historically sticky, and the remaining Nov–Feb window is less favorable for a fully tapered long-course world-record attempt than the summer championship season. I still leave some downside because Finke’s record is only 0.35s under the previous mark and young distance swimmers like Jaouadi/Schwarz could improve suddenly, so the probability declines with horizon.

Liu Zige was still listed by Wikipedia as the women's long-course 200m butterfly record holder as of the 2025-11-04 revision. The main live threat is Summer McIntosh, who at the 2025 World Championships swam 2:01.99, just 0.18 slower than Liu's 2:01.81. That makes the record vulnerable in principle, but the remaining horizon is short and falls after the main long-course championship season, when there are typically fewer top-end long-course record opportunities. So I expect Liu to remain the record holder on all three dates, with probability gradually declining as the forecast horizon lengthens.

My forecast is strongly NO across all three dates. The key fact from Wikipedia is that Hikaru Nakamura was already listed at 2813 on the 1 Nov 2025 FIDE rankings, while the prompt notes a most recently known late-October value of 2816. A 1% increase from that range means roughly 2841-2844, which would be an enormous jump for an already 2800+ established player and likely a major career-high territory. For 2025-11-16, the Wikipedia page will very likely still reflect the 1 Nov list, so a YES would be almost impossible. For 2025-12-09, the page could reflect the 1 Dec list, but gaining ~28-31 Elo in one rating period is still extraordinarily unlikely. For 2026-02-07 there is more time, so uncertainty rises slightly, but the available evidence suggests Nakamura's late-2025 classical activity was aimed at securing the Candidates rating qualification with lower-risk events rather than generating a massive rating spike; that strategy can add some points, but usually not enough to reach 2841-2844. Hence all three are low, with the farthest date a bit closer to 0.5 due to greater horizon uncertainty.

Wikipedia’s List of infectious diseases, as of 2025-10-01, still shows Gonorrhea with no vaccine listed. The prompt also states the most recent known value as of 2025-10-30 was No. The main upside evidence is that GSK has an investigational gonorrhea vaccine with FDA Fast Track designation and an active Phase 1/2 study, plus there is published evidence that MenB-4C may provide partial cross-protection. But that still looks far short of a developed/recognized gonorrhea vaccine by Nov 2025, and there is no sign in the search results of a late-stage pivotal success or imminent approval that would likely cause Wikipedia to switch from No to Yes within days or a few months. So I assign a very low chance for the near dates, rising modestly by Feb 2026 because there is slightly more time for a major update, while keeping the longer-horizon estimate a bit closer to 0.5 as instructed.

The key update is from the Wikipedia page itself: as of the latest revision before the cutoff, the 'Top women' section already lists Lei Tingjie at 2569 on the 1 Nov 2025 FIDE list. That means the 2025-11-09 comparison value is very likely 2569, implying a target of about 2595 to be 1% higher. For 2025-11-16, that is extremely unlikely because the November list is already out and there is essentially no normal monthly-rating mechanism for Wikipedia to show a new higher official rating before then. By 2025-12-09, one new monthly list could have appeared, but a +26 jump from an already elite rating is rare absent evidence of a major successful classical event. By 2026-02-07 there is more time and more monthly-list opportunities, so the probability rises, but it still remains well below even odds because +26 points at this level would be a very large move. If the true 2025-11-09 baseline were somehow 2566 instead of 2569, the hurdle would be slightly easier (~2592), which is why I am not lower on the December/February dates.

The comparison rank on 2025-11-09 is effectively 1, so each later date resolves YES only if Carlsen is still FIDE world #1 on the Wikipedia page then. The pre-cutoff Wikipedia page already shows him #1 on the 1 Nov 2025 list at 2839, with a 26-point cushion over Nakamura and 44 over Caruana. The Wikipedia source page itself showed Enterobiasis with an empty Vaccine(s) field as of the latest pre-cutoff revision, so the starting state is clearly No. Additional medical sources (StatPearls/NIH, CDC, MSD) all describe pinworm as being managed with hygiene and anti-helminthic medication and do not mention any vaccine, while the only Enterobius-specific research I found was basic antigen/diagnostic work rather than a vaccine program. So a flip to Yes within 1 week or 1 month looks extremely unlikely; I raise the probability only slightly by February 2026 because there is more time for an unexpected development or Wikipedia reinterpretation.

As of the 2025-10-01 Wikipedia revision, the vaccine field for 'Calicivirus infection (Norovirus and Sapovirus)' is blank, so the starting state is No. The only credible near-term path to a Yes is norovirus vaccine progress, mainly Moderna’s Phase 3 program. But detailed pre-cutoff reporting indicates there were still no licensed norovirus vaccines, and Moderna’s Phase 3 timing had slipped because case accrual was insufficient, pushing data collection into a second 2025–26 season. That makes a Wikipedia flip within a week or a month extremely unlikely. By 2026-02-07 there is a bit more time for an efficacy readout or an editorial change, but licensure and clear Wikipedia recognition still look unlikely, especially given the combined norovirus/sapovirus row.

Primary anchor is the price-history model: ABBV closed about 215.6 on 2025-11-07, and the yfinance trend analysis estimated roughly 0.44 probability of being above that level by 2025-11-16.
